senses.definition: A genus of small liverwort plants found in moist places, studied in botany.
examples: The professor showed us a specimen of Asterella under the microscope.
Asterella often grows on damp rocks in shaded areas.
Researchers compared Asterella with other liverwort genera in the study.

senses.definition: Plural of astereognosis, meaning conditions where people cannot recognize objects by touch even though they can feel them.
examples: The neurologist described several astereognoses observed in stroke patients.
Different astereognoses may result from damage to different parts of the brain.

senses.definition: A medical condition in which someone cannot recognize familiar objects by touch, even though their sense of touch is normal.
examples: Astereognosis can occur after damage to the parietal lobe of the brain.
The patient showed astereognosis in the left hand but not in the right.
Astereognosis is tested by placing common objects in the patient’s hand with their eyes closed.

senses.definition: A gemstone, such as a sapphire, that shows a star-shaped light pattern when cut in a certain way.
examples: The ring was set with a rare blue asteria.
Under direct light, the asteria displayed a clear six-pointed star.
Collectors pay high prices for fine asteria stones.

senses.definition: Plural of asteria, gemstones that show a star-shaped light effect.
examples: The tray contained several asteriae of different colors.
The jeweler compared the stars in the two asteriae.
High-quality asteriae are rare in the market.

senses.definition: Relating to or shaped like a star.
examples: The mineral showed an asterial pattern under the light.
They admired the asterial glow within the gemstone.
The crystal’s asterial structure fascinated the researchers.

senses.definition: A genus of sea stars, including common starfish species found in cold and temperate seas.
examples: Asterias is often used as a model organism in zoology classes.
We observed Asterias feeding on mussels in the tide pool.
The life cycle of Asterias includes a free-swimming larval stage.

senses.definition: Describing a gemstone that shows a star-shaped light effect inside it.
examples: The jeweler displayed an asteriated sapphire.
Asteriated gems are often cut as cabochons.
Collectors value highly asteriated stones with sharp stars.

senses.definition: A member of a large group of flowering plants that includes many familiar trees, shrubs, and herbs.
examples: Sunflowers and tomatoes are both asterid plants.
The botanist focused her research on asterid diversity.
Asterid species are common in temperate regions.

senses.definition: An echinoderm of the class Asteroidea, such as a starfish.
examples: The museum’s tank contained several asteridians.
Asteridians show a remarkable ability to regenerate lost arms.
The researcher compared brittle stars and asteridians.

senses.definition: Relating to or belonging to the class of starfish.
examples: The asteridian body plan is radially symmetrical.
She published a paper on asteridian anatomy.
Asteridian larvae are planktonic.

senses.definition: Plural of asteridian, meaning several starfish or starfish-like echinoderms.
examples: Asteridians are common in rocky coastal areas.
The study examined how asteridians respond to changes in salinity.
Several asteridians clung to the aquarium glass.

senses.definition: Plural of asterid, referring to many plants in that large group of flowering plants.
examples: Asterids include many important crop plants.
The forest is rich in asterids and rosids.
Scientists are studying how asterids adapted to dry climates.

senses.definition: A family of sea stars that includes the common starfish Asterias rubens.
examples: Asteriidae are widespread in North Atlantic waters.
The family Asteriidae includes several familiar starfish.
Researchers revised the classification of Asteriidae based on DNA data.

senses.definition: An old or specialized term for an asterisk symbol used in texts.
examples: The editor used an asterikos to mark the note.
In the manuscript, an asterikos shows a missing line.
The printer replaced the asterikos with a more modern sign.

senses.definition: A chemical compound or pigment related to star-shaped or star-marked organisms, used in specialized scientific contexts.
examples: The researchers isolated asterin from the marine sample.
Asterin was detected only in the treated cultures.
The team measured the concentration of asterin over time.

senses.definition: A genus of small sea stars that usually live on rocks and seaweed in shallow water.
examples: Asterina species are common on European shores.
We found Asterina under the seaweed at low tide.
The guide pointed out a tiny Asterina clinging to the rock.

senses.definition: A family of small sea stars that often live in shallow coastal waters.
examples: Asterinidae includes several genera of small starfish.
The diversity of Asterinidae is highest in warm seas.
She specialized in the taxonomy of Asterinidae.

senses.definition: Star-shaped or resembling a starfish in form.
examples: The fossil shows an asterioid outline.
Several asterioid patterns appear in the rock layer.
The organism has an asterioid body plan.

senses.definition: In anatomy, a point on the skull where three cranial sutures meet.
examples: The surgeon marked the asterion before the procedure.
The asterion can be felt behind the ear in some patients.
The scan showed an abnormality near the asterion.

senses.definition: A genus of microscopic freshwater algae known as diatoms.
examples: Asterionella often forms star-shaped colonies in lakes.
The scientist identified Asterionella in the water sample.
Blooms of Asterionella can affect water quality.

senses.definition: A small star-shaped mark or symbol used in printing or writing, similar to an asterisk.
examples: An asteriscus was placed at the bottom of the page.
The editor used an asteriscus to indicate a special note.
In old texts, the asteriscus often appears in the margin.

senses.definition: Plural of asteriscus, several small star-shaped marks or symbols used in text.
examples: Three asteriscuses mark the start of the section.
The manuscript shows asteriscuses beside important lines.
The printer removed the extra asteriscuses.

senses.definition: The star-shaped symbol * used in writing or printing, often to show a note or to mark something special.
examples: There is an asterisk next to the difficult words.
Please see the asterisk at the bottom of the page.
Words with an asterisk are explained in the glossary.

senses.definition: A symbol used in linguistics to show that a sentence or form is not acceptable or not actually used.
examples: In the example, the asterisk shows that the sentence is ungrammatical.
Linguists place an asterisk before forms that are not used by native speakers.
The asterisk here marks a reconstructed word.

senses.definition: To mark a word, phrase, or sentence with an asterisk.
examples: The editor asterisked all the key terms.
These examples are asterisked because they are incorrect.
She asterisked the items that need checking.

senses.definition: Marked with an asterisk symbol.
examples: All asterisked items are required.
The asterisked words are explained in the notes.
Please check the asterisked sentences carefully.

senses.definition: Past tense of asterisk: to have marked something with an asterisk symbol.
examples: The teacher asterisked the incorrect answers.
They asterisked the most important points.
He had asterisked several names in the list.

senses.definition: Present participle of asterisk: marking something with an asterisk symbol.
examples: She is asterisking the items that need revision.
By asterisking the key terms, he made them easy to find.
The editor spent the morning asterisking examples in the textbook.

senses.definition: Without any asterisk marks.
examples: This page is completely asteriskless.
He compared the asteriskless version with the annotated one.
An asteriskless list may be easier for beginners to read.

senses.definition: A historical term for an asterisk or small star-shaped mark used in manuscripts.
examples: In the ancient text, an asteriskos marks doubtful lines.
The scholar explained the use of the asteriskos in Greek manuscripts.
An asteriskos appears in the margin beside the correction.

senses.definition: Plural of asterisk, several star-shaped symbols * used in writing or printing.
examples: Three asterisks indicate a section break.
The list uses asterisks instead of numbers.
You can see asterisks beside the most important points.

senses.definition: A pattern of stars in the night sky, which may be part of a constellation or made from parts of different constellations.
examples: The Big Dipper is a well-known asterism in the northern sky.
He taught the children how to find a simple asterism of three bright stars.
This small asterism is visible only in dark conditions.

senses.definition: A printing symbol made of three stars in a triangle (⁂), used to show a break in a text or to draw attention.
examples: The editor used an asterism to separate the two scenes.
An asterism marks the change of topic in this chapter.
Older books sometimes show an asterism instead of a blank line.

senses.definition: A star-shaped light effect seen inside some gemstones when they are cut in a certain way.
examples: The asterism in this sapphire is very clear.
Strong asterism increases the value of the stone.
Under direct light, the asterism appears as a six-pointed star.

senses.definition: Showing or relating to a star-shaped light effect inside a gemstone.
examples: The stone’s asterismal quality makes it unique.
Asterismal sapphires are often cut as cabochons.
She specializes in asterismal gemstones.

senses.definition: Plural of asterism, meaning several star patterns in the sky.
examples: We learned to recognize several asterisms in the summer sky.
Different cultures have named many asterisms.
The guide pointed out a few simple asterisms for beginners.

senses.definition: A mineral or microscopic star-shaped body found in certain crystals or biological structures.
examples: The sample contained tiny asterite crystals.
Asterite structures were visible under the microscope.
The researcher described the asterite as a star-shaped inclusion.

senses.definition: A sudden, brief loss of muscle control, often seen as a flapping movement in the hands, usually caused by serious illness.
examples: The patient showed asterixis when he held out his hands.
Asterixis is often associated with liver failure.
The doctor checked for asterixis during the neurological exam.

senses.definition: Behind or toward the back of a ship or boat.
examples: The small boat was drifting astern.
They checked for obstacles astern before reversing.
A light flashed from somewhere astern.

senses.definition: Not connected to the breastbone (sternum).
examples: The last two ribs are usually asternal.
Asternal ribs do not attach directly to the sternum.
The report focused on asternal vertebrae.

senses.definition: A historical or specialized taxonomic group of animals characterized by lacking a true sternum.
examples: The class Asternata is no longer widely used in modern taxonomy.
Early zoologists placed several reptiles in Asternata.
The paper reviews the history of Asternata in classification.

senses.definition: A rare condition in which the breastbone (sternum) is missing or not fully formed.
examples: Asternia is usually identified at birth.
The surgeons discussed options for treating asternia.
Asternia can be associated with other chest abnormalities.

senses.definition: A genus of small marine mollusks (chitons) with star-like markings on their shells.
examples: Asterochiton species live on rocky shores.
The museum has a rare specimen of Asterochiton.
She described a new Asterochiton in her paper.

senses.definition: A small rocky object that moves around the sun, mostly between Mars and Jupiter.
examples: Scientists discovered a new asteroid near Mars.
The asteroid passed safely at a great distance from Earth.
Many asteroids are found in the asteroid belt.

senses.definition: Relating to or like an asteroid.
examples: The probe entered an asteroidal orbit.
They studied asteroidal material collected by the spacecraft.
The region is filled with asteroidal debris.

senses.definition: The class of echinoderms that includes sea stars (starfish).
examples: Asteroidea includes many familiar starfish.
Students learned about the characteristics of Asteroidea.
Asteroidea is one of the main groups of echinoderms.

senses.definition: Relating to or belonging to the class Asteroidea (sea stars).
examples: The asteroidean body is typically star-shaped.
Asteroidean larvae usually live in the plankton.
She wrote a paper on asteroidean development.

senses.definition: An animal belonging to the class Asteroidea, such as a sea star.
examples: Several asteroideans were found attached to the rocks.
Asteroideans are important predators in many marine ecosystems.
The aquarium displays different asteroideans from around the world.

senses.definition: Plural of asteroidean, meaning several sea stars of the class Asteroidea.
examples: Asteroideans feed on mollusks and other invertebrates.
The reef hosts many asteroideans and sea urchins.
Researchers tagged a number of asteroideans for the study.

senses.definition: Plural of asteroid, many small rocky objects moving around the sun.
examples: Thousands of asteroids orbit between Mars and Jupiter.
Some asteroids come close to Earth.
The spacecraft sent back images of several asteroids.

senses.definition: An extinct family of prehistoric jawless fishes known from fossils.
examples: Asterolepidae are known mainly from Devonian rocks.
The museum displays several fossils from Asterolepidae.
Researchers revised the classification of Asterolepidae.

senses.definition: A genus of extinct armored fishes from the Devonian period.
examples: The fossil clearly belonged to Asterolepis.
Asterolepis had a heavily armored head and body.
They found a new species of Asterolepis in the quarry.

senses.definition: The name of a star in the Pleiades cluster, or a name used in classical mythology.
examples: Asterope is one of the faint stars in the Pleiades.
The story of Asterope appears in ancient Greek myths.
Through the telescope, he could just see Asterope.

senses.definition: A brown or yellowish mineral that forms star-like crystal groups, often found in igneous rocks.
examples: The sample contained veins of asterophyllite.
Asterophyllite crystals formed radiating clusters.
Collectors prize well-formed asterophyllite specimens.

senses.definition: A genus of extinct fossil plants with narrow leaves arranged in star-like whorls.
examples: Asterophyllites fossils were common in the coal measures.
The paleobotanist identified the leaf as Asterophyllites.
Asterophyllites helps scientists understand ancient swamp forests.

senses.definition: An old taxonomic group of fishes characterized by star-like structures in their vertebrae.
examples: Asterospondyli were recognized in early fish classifications.
The study revisits the concept of Asterospondyli.
Fossils assigned to Asterospondyli show distinctive vertebrae.
